award_name: Manufacturing and Associated Industries and Occupations Award MA000010
status: PREVIEW — machine-validated from FWO Pay Guide (published 24 Jun 2026, effective 1 Jul 2026); gated behind manufacturing_preview flag, not GA
source: Fair Work Ombudsman Pay Guide MA000010, published 24 June 2026
coverage_note: General manufacturing only. Does NOT cover vehicle manufacturing (Schedule B of MA000010).
minimum_engagement_note: Part-time: minimum 4 consecutive hours per shift (clause 10.2); casual: minimum 4 consecutive hours per attendance (clause 11.2). Both may be reduced to no less than 3 consecutive hours by written agreement at the employee's request. Full-time has no per-shift statutory minimum (ordinary hours average 38/week). Source: MA000010 award text.
penalty_rates_source: Saturday/Sunday/public holiday and overtime per the rates schedule; shift loadings confirmed against award clause 33.2 — afternoon/night shift = 115% (clause 33.2(d)), permanent night shift = 130% (clause 33.2(f)).

supervisor_trainer_coordinator.level_I: max(122% of the minimum hourly rate of the highest technically qualified employee supervised/trained, 104.3% of the standard rate)
supervisor_trainer_coordinator.level_II: max(115% of the minimum hourly rate of the highest paid employee supervised/trained, 113.1% of the standard rate)
supervisor_trainer_coordinator.technical: paid an allowance under clause 30.2(f) instead of the clause 20.1(g) rates
supervisor_trainer_coordinator.note: Relational rates — computed from the supervised employee's rate or the standard rate (the C10 rate), whichever is higher. Not absolute-rated.

notes: General manufacturing classifications C14-C2(b) plus professional/technical, junior, apprentice, trainee and cadet streams.
Supervisor/Trainer/Coordinator is a relational (percentage) classification — see formula_classifications.
Shift loadings confirmed against MA000010 clause 33.2.

Apprentice rates for apprenticeships commenced before 1 January 2014 are no longer published in the FWO Pay Guide (FWO directs to the Pay and Conditions Tool); those 24 legacy rows were removed in the 2026-07 update. All published General Manufacturing rates re-extracted and validated (145/145: hourly*38==weekly, Sat 1.5 / Sun 2.0 / PH 2.5).
